title: Piquant Brazilian Cheese Croquette
description: Resinous, cheese-pull croquettes with scallions and deep-fried to crispy perfection, a nostalgic Brazilian snack.
instructions: Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat until foamy.
Whisk in flour and cook for 2 minutes until a smooth roux forms.
Gradually whisk in milk, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens to a pastry consistency.
Remove the pan from heat and stir in grated Gruyere, chopped scallions, salt, and pepper until the cheese melts and the dough is glossy.
Transfer the dough to a bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and chill in the refrigerator for 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a deep pan to 180°C.
Shape chilled dough into 1 tablespoon-sized oblong rolls.
Fry rolls in batches for 3 to 4 minutes, turning once, until golden brown.
Remove croquettes with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Serve hot, optionally with spicy tomato sauce and a sprinkle of fresh parsley.
